hypothesis: If the β parameter in DPO controls the degree of distributional shift from the SFT policy, then dynamically scheduling β across training—starting high to preserve chemical grammar learned during SFT and gradually decreasing to allow greater reward-driven exploration—will simultaneously prevent the over-steering failure mode observed in OLMo and yield larger mean reward improvements than fixed-β DPO across all three model architectures, without sacrificing coverage recall or generated pair uniqueness.
